beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: 24-hour shift rotation means Mia is off today after yesterday's station duty; she wakes refreshed but groggy. Morning routine is deliberate—she's meticulous about prep.

beats.activity: Check weather and plan the day; review vinyl collection spreadsheet while rain patters outside.
beats.reasoning: Rain has started; Mia is meticulous and checks conditions before committing to errands. She's in a relaxed post-shift mood (energy 60/100), so she lingers at home with a hobby.

beats.activity: Drive to Whole Foods Market to pick up brewing supplies and groceries, navigating light rain.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure won't affect a local errand; Mia heads to nearby Roosevelt via surface streets. Rain is steady but manageable for a quick run.

beats.activity: Shop for home brewing ingredients, grains, and fresh produce for the week.
beats.reasoning: Mia is a serious home brewer and meticulous planner; she takes time to select quality ingredients. Rain outside keeps her focused on the task.

beats.activity: Drive back home, rain still steady.
beats.reasoning: Quick return to Eastlake; SR-520 closure eastbound doesn't affect westbound Seattle traffic, so no major delays.

beats.activity: Unpack groceries, prep home brewing setup, and organize supplies in the garage.
beats.reasoning: Off-shift energy and rainy weather keep Mia content indoors; home brewing is her passion and a competitive outlet. She's meticulous about process and takes her time.

beats.activity: Make lunch—a simple sandwich and soup, eaten at home while rain continues.
beats.reasoning: Rain is ongoing; Mia stays in. She values efficiency and comfort, preparing a quick meal to fuel an afternoon of projects.

beats.activity: Continue brewing prep and listen to vinyl records; rain is clearing.
beats.reasoning: By early afternoon, rain is tapering off. Mia settles into her favorite hobby—spinning vinyl while tending to her brewing rig. Competitive and detail-oriented, she enjoys the meditative rhythm.

beats.activity: Text a close friend and arrange an early-evening walk to Gas Works Park to catch the clearing skies.; Walk around Gas Works Park with a friend, enjoying the break in rain and views of Lake Union.
beats.reasoning: Mia values time with close friends over crowds; weather is improving. A walk clears her head after shift fatigue and indoor projects.

beats.activity: Drive back home from Gas Works Park.
beats.reasoning: Short drive back to Eastlake; evening is approaching and Mia is settling in for the night.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a simple dinner, review expense spreadsheet, and relax with a TV show or more vinyl.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down at home. Mia is meticulous with finances (spreadsheet review), then unwinds with leisure. Off-shift day allows for a relaxed evening.

beats.activity: Sleep, getting solid rest before returning to shift rotation.
beats.reasoning: 8 hours of sleep allows recovery from yesterday's 24-hour shift and prepares Mia for the rotation cycle ahead. Sleep spans overnight as required.

diary: Woke up to that familiar grey Seattle drizzle—perfect coffee weather. Spent the morning in my own little world: checked the vinyl spreadsheet, then headed to Whole Foods in Roosevelt to grab brewing supplies. The rain was steady but manageable, though I heard the bridge was a mess eastbound. Glad I stayed on this side of the lake. Got home, set up my brewing station in the garage, made a simple lunch. There's something grounding about prepping that setup, listening to records while the rain softened. Mid-afternoon, texted my friend and we met at Gas Works Park—the clouds were finally breaking, and the light off the lake was beautiful. We walked for a bit, didn't talk about much, just needed the air. Back home for dinner, caught up on some shows, reviewed bills. Now I'm heading into my 24-hour shift tomorrow. Tonight feels full in a quiet way.
thought_process: Today was one of those days where I needed to stay close to home and process things at my own pace. The grey weather actually suited my mood—no pressure to be anywhere urgent. I deliberately avoided the bridge chaos by staying local; at my age, I'm learning that fighting traffic isn't worth it. The brewing prep was meditative, something I can control and perfect. Reaching out to my friend felt important; I've been doing a lot of solo reflection lately, and sometimes you just need someone beside you, even in silence. The walk cleared my head before the shift rotation. I'm going into tomorrow's 24 hours rested and grounded.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
